globalAlerts.warning: ON TOOLS: Use `config.mk` to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that *limit* the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can *search* the output. To be clear, timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Testing retries, for example, MUST be done in a way that supports avoiding running afoul of those CRITICAL rules. Abide. OBEY.

mandatoryDirectives.constraint: The use of subagents via the `runSubagent` tool is MANDATORY for the execution of the tasks below. Tasks are grouped specifically to be run in distinct subagent contexts to prevent context window exhaustion. Do not attempt to solve all groups in a single turn.

statusSection.currentState: T001-T076 completed (coverage audits, API optimization, profiling, security, bug fixes, features). T077 (Windows) In Progress — code changes committed (307165b), actual Windows test run pending. T078-T102 planned: platform verification, documentation overhaul, CHANGELOG, shell completion, QoL features, session cleanup, dependency audit, final integration, scope expansion.

sequentialTasks.outcome: 70.2%→84.0%. 18 new tests. Remaining gaps are platform branches.
14 integration tests covering all REPL commands.
0%→98.7%. 39 tests from scratch. Fixed 2 nil-guard bugs.
template 80.9→95.7%, unicodetext 92.5→100%, etc. ~42 tests. 6 bug fixes.

4 fuzz tests. Zero panics across 2.4M+ execs.
macOS <10% variance. Linux 400x-20,000x headroom. Windows deferred to T077.

sequentialTasks.outcome: Already fully implemented. No changes needed.

sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make make-all-run-windows' (host 'moo'). Fix Windows-specific failures. Verify platform paths (session_windows.go, registry_windows.go, atomic_write_windows.go, filelock_windows.go). Verify benchmark thresholds (deferred from T061). Zero failures.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Fixed 2 failures: echo builtin skip, tview Console API skip. All 3 platforms pass.

sequentialTasks.description: Run 'make all' on macOS. Verify session_other.go paths, clipboard ops (pbcopy), PTY tests, symlink resolution (/private/var/folders), SkipIfRoot tests run normally. Zero failures. This establishes the clean baseline before documentation and feature work.
sequentialTasks.outcome: make all passes with zero failures on macOS. Clean baseline established.

sequentialTasks.description: The current [Unreleased] section has only 6 entries despite 56 completed tasks (T021-T076). Add ALL missing entries using Keep a Changelog 1.1.0 format. Categories: Added (coverage tests, fuzz, benchmarks, security tests, config persistence, hot-snippets, completions, prompt-flow auto-generate, txtar LCA, goal params, PostCopyHint, goals, scriptCommandBase, RefreshPath normalization), Changed (API surface: unexported 16+ symbols, pabt.ModuleLoader→Require, BTBridge removal, CONFIG_HOT_SNIPPETS moved), Fixed (data race, %v→%w, nil guards, TOCTOU, SkipIfRoot, flaky BT tests), Removed (deprecated aliases, ContextCommand, BTBridge alias, unused constants, stale .deadcodeignore).
sequentialTasks.outcome: Rewrote [Unreleased] with 16 Added, 4 Changed, 6 Removed, 13 Fixed entries. Fixed v0.1.0 format.

sequentialTasks.task: T080: Documentation: scripting.md — complete module reference
sequentialTasks.description: Update module reference table for all 22 registered modules in register.go. For EACH module, document: name, exported functions/methods, parameter types, return types, examples. Fill gaps: osm:grpc full API, osm:sharedStateSymbols, remove TODO markers, document osm:ctxutil factory/REPL, document args global, fix createAdvancedPrompt refs, verify osm:bt and osm:pabt sections.
sequentialTasks.outcome: All 20 modules documented in categorized tables. No deprecated refs. Module resolution documented.

sequentialTasks.task: T081: Documentation: reference/command.md — add missing commands
sequentialTasks.description: Add: osm sync (init/push/pull/save/list), osm log (tail [-n/-f]), osm super-document (-i/-test/-session/-store). Update: osm config (now PERSISTENT per T065, schema-aware validation, validate/schema subcommands). Verify all command docs match current SetupFlags implementations.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Added sync/log/super-document sections. Updated config persistence. Verified all SetupFlags.

sequentialTasks.task: T082: Documentation: reference/config.md — all config keys from schema.go
sequentialTasks.description: Cross-reference schema.go DefaultSchema() with config.md. Add ALL missing config keys with type, default, description, env var override. Add [hot-snippets] section format documentation. Update env variables section.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Documented all 35 global schema keys, 14 command-section keys, hot-snippets format, env vars.

sequentialTasks.outcome: Added bannerTemplate, usageTemplate, hotSnippets, flagDefs, .prompt.md discovery, 10-goal catalog.

sequentialTasks.task: T084: Documentation: session.md — update lifecycle details
sequentialTasks.description: Document: session delete (-dry-run/-y), session info, session list flags, SessionConfig reserved fields, cross-reference auto-determination docs, verify storage backend docs.
sequentialTasks.outcome: All 7 subcommands documented, cleanup config table, storage backends expanded.

sequentialTasks.task: T085: Documentation: configuration.md — add new config sections
sequentialTasks.description: Add: [hot-snippets] section, Logging (log.*), Sync (sync.*), prompt.file-paths, script.module-paths, updated env vars. Ensure consistency with reference/config.md.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Added hot-snippets, logging, sync, prompt.file-paths, script.module-paths sections. Env vars restructured.

sequentialTasks.description: Expand from ~30-line skeleton to cover: entry point wiring, command system (BaseCommand, scriptCommandBase), scripting engine (lifecycle, EngineOption, event loop, require, globals), 22 builtin modules by category, session/storage, configuration (dnsmasq-style, schema, env vars, persistence), goal system, sync subsystem, data flow diagram.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Replaced skeleton with comprehensive doc: entry point, commands, scripting engine, 20 modules, session, config, goals, sync, data flow diagram.

continuousVerification.task: Run 'make all' with full logging after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description: Execute 'make make-all-with-log' after: (a) Each code change, (b) Each test addition, (c) Each task completion, (d) Each bug fix.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: You will ALWAYS aim for 100% effective test coverage, and VERIFY test coverage by means of the tooling available to you. You will NOT proceed to the next task until you have achieved 100% effective test coverage for the current task, and have verified that coverage with the tools available to you.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ON TOOLS: Use `config.mk` to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that *limit* the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can *search* the output. To be clear, timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Testing retries, for example, MUST be done in a way that supports avoiding running afoul of those CRITICAL rules. Abide. OBEY.
